title: Why l. | l
excerpt: Twenty-five years with k and q. I did not want a new language. I wanted to rebuild the engine underneath it.
content_html:
I have been working with K and Q since 2001, nearly twenty-five years now. I will say the obvious part first: Arthur Whitney is a genius.

K and Q remain extraordinary languages. Their design is small, dense, and beautiful. But despite years of optimization, extension, and correction, the core implementation model is still rooted in Arthur’s original insight from the 1990s.

I did not want to change the language. I am not arrogant enough to think I should invent a new one. What I wanted to do was reimagine the implementation for the machines we actually use today.

Computers in 2026 are not the computers of 1997. CPUs are wider. Memory is relatively slower. Every machine has multiple cores. Many have GPUs, NPUs, AMX units, NEON, AVX, VNNI, and other specialized execution engines. The hardware has changed dramatically. The programming model has not kept up.

l is my attempt to keep the language, K, Q, qSQL, IPC, the whole world of existing code, and rebuild the execution engine underneath it. The implementation is built around four ideas: SIMD, parallelism, fusion, and compression.

SIMD as a first-class primitive

I wanted every operator to take advantage of the hardware automatically. Before clusters, GPUs, or distributed systems, a simpler question: how fast can every primitive be on a single core?

That means making every cycle count. It means treating SIMD not as an optional optimization but as the default path for primitive execution. If the hardware can process many values per instruction, the language should use that automatically, no rewrites, no vector intrinsics, no thinking about it. The primitive should just be fast.

Parallelism by default

Parallelism has always been natural for array languages. APL, K, and Q all express work as large, regular operations over arrays, exactly the structure modern CPUs can exploit.

But I wanted to think about it from first principles. Instead of bolting threads on as an external tool, l treats parallel execution as part of the primitive runtime. Small arrays stay on the fast single-core SIMD path. Larger arrays split across worker threads automatically. The user never decides when to parallelize; the runtime decides, from the size, shape, and cost of the operation.

And as arrays get larger still, the same idea extends to accelerators. On a modern Apple machine, many workloads are too small to justify moving data to a GPU, but some are not. Small values stay on CPU pipelines. Larger values use threads. The largest operations move to a GPU, NPU, or other backend when it pays. You should not need a different version of the language because the hardware changed.

Transparent fusion

When I wrote K and Q in financial services, the language was usually elegant. When it became slow, the cause was rarely the primitive itself. It was intermediate allocation.

You write something beautiful:

q) sum sqrt x

Conceptually simple: square-root each value, then sum. But a traditional implementation may allocate an entire intermediate vector for sqrt x, only to consume it immediately in sum. That allocation is waste.

l makes fusion transparent. The runtime chains primitive operations and eliminates intermediate objects wherever it can. In sum sqrt x, the summing loop consumes the square-rooted values directly. Where safe, it modifies temporaries in place. Where possible, it collapses the whole expression into one pipeline. You still write normal K or Q. The implementation makes the obvious thing fast.

Compression as a runtime concept

The fourth idea, the hardest, and probably the most important, is compression. It should not be a storage feature off to the side. It should be a first-class concept inside the runtime.

An operator should not care whether the object it operates on is compressed. The runtime should know how to operate on compressed data directly. For compression, SIMD, threading, and fusion to compose correctly, they have to be designed together. That is the core implementation challenge.

With few exceptions, most primitives can work directly on compressed data. A huge vector can stay compressed, move fewer bytes through memory, fit better in cache, and still produce the same result.

Memory bandwidth is the enemy. Modern CPUs are incredibly fast. The bottleneck is rarely arithmetic. It is moving data: RAM to cache, cache to registers, core to core, CPU to accelerator.

The simplest way to make software faster is to move less data over a shorter distance. That is why compression is central to l. If a vector can be represented in fewer bytes, l keeps it that way. If the data can stay in cache, it stays in cache. If an operation can execute without decompressing the full vector, it does. The runtime moves the minimum number of bytes required to compute the correct answer.

The guardrail: full K and Q compatibility

The guardrail for all of this was compatibility. I wanted l to be K and Q, not “inspired by K,” not “similar to Q.” I wanted existing commercial k4 and Q code to run without modification. I did not want to leave behind more than two decades of code, puzzles, and a community I grew up with.

That means implementing the language, qSQL, IPC, and the surrounding execution model, and taking real code, not just examples, and asking whether it can actually run inside a larger system. Once you commit to that, the problem becomes much harder, and much more meaningful.

The point of l is not to create a new language and ask people to migrate. The point is to preserve the language and replace the machinery underneath it. Same language. New engine.

K and Q gave us one of the most powerful array programming models ever built. l asks what that model looks like when the implementation is designed for modern hardware from the ground up: SIMD by default, parallel by default, fused by default, and compressed by default.

That is why I built l.

sentences.en: The implementation is built around four ideas: SIMD, parallelism, fusion, and compression.
sentences.zh: 该实现围绕四个理念构建：SIMD、并行性、融合和压缩。

sentences.zh: 在集群、GPU 或分布式系统之前，有一个更简单的问题：每个原语在单个核心上的速度有多快？

sentences.zh: 这意味着让每个周期都有意义。

sentences.zh: 这意味着不要将 SIMD 视为可选优化，而是将其视为原语执行的默认路径。

sentences.en: If the hardware can process many values per instruction, the language should use that automatically, no rewrites, no vector intrinsics, no thinking about it.
sentences.zh: 如果硬件可以在每条指令上处理许多值，则语言应该自动使用它，无需重写，无需向量内在函数，无需考虑它。

sentences.en: The primitive should just be fast.
sentences.zh: 原语应该很快。

sentences.zh: l 没有将线程作为外部工具，而是将并行执行视为原始运行时的一部分。

sentences.en: Small arrays stay on the fast single-core SIMD path.
sentences.zh: 小型阵列仍采用快速单核 SIMD 路径。

sentences.en: Larger arrays split across worker threads automatically.
sentences.zh: 较大的数组会自动分割到工作线程中。

sentences.en: The user never decides when to parallelize; the runtime decides, from the size, shape, and cost of the operation.
sentences.zh: 用户永远不会决定何时并行化；运行时由操作的大小、形状和成本决定。

sentences.zh: 在现代 Apple 机器上，许多工作负载都太小，无法将数据移动到 GPU，但有些则不然。

sentences.en: Small values stay on CPU pipelines.
sentences.zh: 小值保留在 CPU 管道上。

sentences.en: Larger values use threads.
sentences.zh: 较大的值使用线程。

sentences.en: The largest operations move to a GPU, NPU, or other backend when it pays.
sentences.zh: 当需要付费时，最大的运算会转移到 GPU、NPU 或其他后端。

sentences.en: You should not need a different version of the language because the hardware changed.
sentences.zh: 您不应该因为硬件发生变化而需要不同版本的语言。

sentences.en: Conceptually simple: square-root each value, then sum.

sentences.en: But a traditional implementation may allocate an entire intermediate vector for sqrt x, only to consume it immediately in sum.
sentences.zh: 但是传统的实现可能会为 sqrt x 分配整个中间向量，只是为了立即将其消耗掉。

sentences.en: That allocation is waste.
sentences.zh: 这种分配是浪费。

In sum sqrt x, the summing loop consumes the square-rooted values directly. Where safe, it modifies temporaries in place. Where possible, it collapses the whole expression into one pipeline. sentences.zh: l 使融合变得透明。

sentences.en: The runtime chains primitive operations and eliminates intermediate objects wherever it can.
sentences.zh: 运行时会链接原始操作并尽可能消除中间对象。

sentences.en: In sum sqrt x, the summing loop consumes the square-rooted values directly.
sentences.zh: 在 sum sqrt x 中，求和循环直接消耗平方根值。

sentences.en: Where safe, it modifies temporaries in place.
sentences.zh: 在安全的情况下，它会就地修改临时变量。

sentences.en: Where possible, it collapses the whole expression into one pipeline.
sentences.zh: 在可能的情况下，它将整个表达式折叠到一个管道中。

sentences.en: You still write normal K or Q.
sentences.zh: 你还是写普通的K或者Q吧。

sentences.en: The implementation makes the obvious thing fast.
sentences.zh: 实施使显而易见的事情变得更快。

sentences.en: The runtime should know how to operate on compressed data directly.
sentences.zh: 运行时应该知道如何直接操作压缩数据。

sentences.en: For compression, SIMD, threading, and fusion to compose correctly, they have to be designed together.

sentences.zh: 一个巨大的向量可以保持压缩状态，在内存中移动更少的字节，更好地适应缓存，并且仍然产生相同的结果。

The runtime moves the minimum number of bytes required to compute the correct answer.

sentences.en: The simplest way to make software faster is to move less data over a shorter distance.
sentences.zh: 提高软件速度的最简单方法是在更短的距离内移动更少的数据。

sentences.en: That is why compression is central to l.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么压缩对于 l 至关重要。

sentences.en: If a vector can be represented in fewer bytes, l keeps it that way.
sentences.zh: 如果向量可以用更少的字节表示，则 l 会保持这种方式。

sentences.en: If the data can stay in cache, it stays in cache.
sentences.zh: 如果数据可以保留在缓存中，则它会保留在缓存中。

sentences.zh: 如果一个操作可以在不解压缩完整向量的情况下执行，那么它就可以执行。

sentences.en: The runtime moves the minimum number of bytes required to compute the correct answer.
sentences.zh: 运行时移动计算正确答案所需的最小字节数。

sentences.zh: 所有这一切的护栏是兼容性。

sentences.en: I wanted l to be K and Q, not “inspired by K,” not “similar to Q.”
sentences.zh: 我希望 l 成为 K 和 Q，而不是“受 K 启发”，而不是“类似于 Q”。

sentences.en: I wanted existing commercial k4 and Q code to run without modification.
sentences.zh: 我希望现有的商业 k4 和 Q 代码无需修改即可运行。

sentences.en: I did not want to leave behind more than two decades of code, puzzles, and a community I grew up with.
sentences.zh: 我不想留下二十多年的代码、谜题和伴随我成长的社区。

blocks.html_en: That means implementing the language, qSQL, IPC, and the surrounding execution model, and taking real code, not just examples, and asking whether it can actually run inside a larger system. Once you commit to that, the problem becomes much harder, and much more meaningful.

sentences.en: That means implementing the language, qSQL, IPC, and the surrounding execution model, and taking real code, not just examples, and asking whether it can actually run inside a larger system.
sentences.zh: 这意味着实现语言、qSQL、IPC 和周围的执行模型，并采用真实的代码（而不仅仅是示例），并询问它是否可以在更大的系统中实际运行。

sentences.en: Once you commit to that, the problem becomes much harder, and much more meaningful.
sentences.zh: 一旦你致力于这一点，问题就会变得更加困难，也更加有意义。

blocks.html_en: The point of l is not to create a new language and ask people to migrate. The point is to preserve the language and replace the machinery underneath it. Same language. New engine.

sentences.en: The point of l is not to create a new language and ask people to migrate.
sentences.zh: l 的目的不是创造一种新的语言并要求人们迁移。

sentences.en: The point is to preserve the language and replace the machinery underneath it.
sentences.zh: 重点是保留语言并更换其底层的机制。

sentences.en: Same language.
sentences.zh: 相同的语言。

sentences.en: New engine.
sentences.zh: 新发动机。

blocks.html_en: K and Q gave us one of the most powerful array programming models ever built. l asks what that model looks like when the implementation is designed for modern hardware from the ground up: SIMD by default, parallel by default, fused by default, and compressed by default.

sentences.en: K and Q gave us one of the most powerful array programming models ever built.
sentences.zh: K 和 Q 为我们提供了有史以来最强大的数组编程模型之一。

sentences.en: l asks what that model looks like when the implementation is designed for modern hardware from the ground up: SIMD by default, parallel by default, fused by default, and compressed by default.
sentences.zh: l 询问当从头开始为现代硬件设计实现时，该模型是什么样子的：默认为 SIMD、默认为并行、默认为融合、默认为压缩。

blocks.html_en: That is why I built l.

sentences.en: That is why I built l.
sentences.zh: 这就是我建造 l 的原因。

content_html_zh:
我从 2001 年开始与 K 和 Q 合作，至今已有近 25 年了。 我首先要说显而易见的部分：阿瑟惠特尼是一个天才。

K 和 Q 仍然是非凡的语言。 他们的设计小巧、密集且美观。 但尽管经过多年的优化、扩展和修正，核心实现模型仍然植根于 Arthur 20 世纪 90 年代的原始见解。

我不想改变语言。 我还没有傲慢到认为我应该发明一种新的。 我想做的是重新构想我们今天实际使用的机器的实现。

2026 年的计算机不是 1997 年的计算机。 CPU 更宽。 内存相对较慢。 每台机器都有多个核心。 许多拥有 GPU、NPU、AMX 单元、NEON、AVX、VNNI 和其他专用执行引擎。 硬件发生了巨大变化。 编程模型没有跟上。

l 是我尝试保留语言、K、Q、qSQL、IPC、现有代码的整个世界，并在其下面重建执行引擎。 该实现围绕四个理念构建：SIMD、并行性、融合和压缩。

SIMD 作为一流的原语

我希望每个操作员都能自动利用硬件。 在集群、GPU 或分布式系统之前，有一个更简单的问题：每个原语在单个核心上的速度有多快？

这意味着让每个周期都有意义。 这意味着不要将 SIMD 视为可选优化，而是将其视为原语执行的默认路径。 如果硬件可以在每条指令上处理许多值，则语言应该自动使用它，无需重写，无需向量内在函数，无需考虑它。 原语应该很快。

默认并行度

对于数组语言来说，并行性一直是很自然的。 APL、K 和 Q 都将工作表示为对数组的大型常规操作，这正是现代 CPU 可以利用的结构。

但我想从首要原则来思考这个问题。 l 没有将线程作为外部工具，而是将并行执行视为原始运行时的一部分。 小型阵列仍采用快速单核 SIMD 路径。 较大的数组会自动分割到工作线程中。 用户永远不会决定何时并行化；运行时由操作的大小、形状和成本决定。

随着阵列变得越来越大，同样的想法也扩展到了加速器。 在现代 Apple 机器上，许多工作负载都太小，无法将数据移动到 GPU，但有些则不然。 小值保留在 CPU 管道上。 较大的值使用线程。 当需要付费时，最大的运算会转移到 GPU、NPU 或其他后端。 您不应该因为硬件发生变化而需要不同版本的语言。

透明融合

当我在金融服务领域写 K 和 Q 时，语言通常很优雅。 当它变慢时，原因很少是基元本身。 这是中间分配。

你写了一些美丽的东西：

q) sum sqrt x

概念上很简单：对每个值求平方根，然后求和。 但是传统的实现可能会为 sqrt x 分配整个中间向量，只是为了立即将其消耗掉。 这种分配是浪费。

l 使融合变得透明。 运行时会链接原始操作并尽可能消除中间对象。 在 sum sqrt x 中，求和循环直接消耗平方根值。 在安全的情况下，它会就地修改临时变量。 在可能的情况下，它将整个表达式折叠到一个管道中。 你还是写普通的K或者Q吧。 实施使显而易见的事情变得更快。

压缩作为运行时概念

第四个想法是最难的，也可能是最重要的，那就是压缩。 它不应该是一个放在一边的存储功能。 它应该是运行时内的一流概念。

操作员不应该关心其操作的对象是否被压缩。 运行时应该知道如何直接操作压缩数据。 为了正确组合压缩、SIMD、线程和融合，必须将它们设计在一起。 这是核心的实施挑战。

除了少数例外，大多数原语都可以直接处理压缩数据。 一个巨大的向量可以保持压缩状态，在内存中移动更少的字节，更好地适应缓存，并且仍然产生相同的结果。

内存带宽是敌人。 现代 CPU 的速度快得令人难以置信。 瓶颈很少是算术。 它正在移动数据：RAM 到缓存、缓存到寄存器、核心到核心、CPU 到加速器。

提高软件速度的最简单方法是在更短的距离内移动更少的数据。 这就是为什么压缩对于 l 至关重要。 如果向量可以用更少的字节表示，则 l 会保持这种方式。 如果数据可以保留在缓存中，则它会保留在缓存中。 如果一个操作可以在不解压缩完整向量的情况下执行，那么它就可以执行。 运行时移动计算正确答案所需的最小字节数。

护栏：完全 K 和 Q 兼容性

所有这一切的护栏是兼容性。 我希望 l 成为 K 和 Q，而不是“受 K 启发”，而不是“类似于 Q”。 我希望现有的商业 k4 和 Q 代码无需修改即可运行。 我不想留下二十多年的代码、谜题和伴随我成长的社区。

这意味着实现语言、qSQL、IPC 和周围的执行模型，并采用真实的代码（而不仅仅是示例），并询问它是否可以在更大的系统中实际运行。 一旦你致力于这一点，问题就会变得更加困难，也更加有意义。

l 的目的不是创造一种新的语言并要求人们迁移。 重点是保留语言并更换其底层的机制。 相同的语言。 新发动机。

K 和 Q 为我们提供了有史以来最强大的数组编程模型之一。 l 询问当从头开始为现代硬件设计实现时，该模型是什么样子的：默认为 SIMD、默认为并行、默认为融合、默认为压缩。

这就是我建造 l 的原因。
